title: sarothamnus scoparius subsp. andreanus
intro: Sarothamnus scoparius subsp. andreanus, also known as the alpine broom or Scottish broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sarothamnus scoparius subsp. andreanus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
